meta_title: Scrybe — Local subtitles for your own broadcasts
meta_description: Scrybe runs local Speech-To-Text models on your machine to create live captions that you can add to OBS as a browser source. Free, Fully local, open-source, no cloud.
hero_headline: Local subtitles for your own broadcasts.
hero_subtitle: Scrybe runs local Speech-To-Text models on your machine to create live captions that you can add to OBS as a browser source. Free, Fully local, open-source, no cloud.
hero_subtitle_caution: Still in early development, expect lots of changes.
hero_caption_float: and this is what it looks like on stream
feat_local_body: All processing happens on your machine. Your audio never leaves your computer. This means no API keys, no cloud latency, no subscriptions.
feat_audio_body: Select any audio device. Caption dekstop audio, your voice through your microphone, even virtual audio cables.
feat_obs_body_post: into OBS and the subtitles appear. Adjust text alignment, color, font size, transparency, etc and the overlay will update automatically.
feat_settings_body: Swap models, toggle translate, trim silence, etc. Works great out of the box, but allows you to customize your experience.
planned_body: Scrybe is still early, and the roadmap is focused on making live captions more flexible, readable, and useful after the stream.
planned_audio_body: Choose the exact audio source, whether that's a microphone, desktop audio, or audio from a specific process. Follow the system default when it changes, and keep transcription running when it switches.
planned_transcripts_body: More natural caption segments with silence and speaker-aware breaks, steadier updates, accurate timestamps, and speaker identification.
planned_obs_body: A segment-log overlay, saved custom overlay colors, and dedicated OBS plugin support for Twitch native captions and microphone mute events.
planned_control_body: Save transcripts to disk, dedicated translation support, and transcript history.
how_heading: Four steps from install to on-stream subtitles.
how_body: Scrybe is a desktop app based on Tauri, install it like any normal program. Choose from a set of model presets (or bring your own) and start transcribing.
how_step_2: Customize the subtitle box's positioning, color, and size.
whisper_stat_cost_label: no API bills, no account creation
whisper_body_p1_pre: whisper.cpp is Georgi Gerganov's port of OpenAI's Whisper speech-recognition model to plain C/C++. Same models, same weights, it's just stripped of the Python stack and compiled down to something small enough to ship inside a desktop app.
whisper_body_p2_pre: Scrybe embeds it directly. Audio goes in, text comes out, nothing is sent remotely. You pick the model size
whisper_body_p2_post: for when you want more accuracy.
